decks.notes: The main strategy of this deck is to control the game by utilizing lands and disruptive spells to slow down the opponent's game plan. By utilizing cards like Life from the Loam, Wasteland, and Ghost Quarter, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's resources while ramping up its own mana base. The deck also looks to create a powerful token creature with Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage combo. Overall, the deck focuses on controlling the game through land manipulation and disruption, while setting up powerful win conditions.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt and control the opponent through the use of artifacts and prison elements. By utilizing cards like Trinisphere, Pithing Needle, and Karn, the Great Creator, the deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan and lock them out of certain strategies. With powerful artifacts like Candelabra of Tawnos and Grim Monolith, the deck can generate mana quickly to deploy threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, Eye of the Storms. Overall, the deck aims to control the game through a combination of disruption and powerful artifact synergies.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifact cards to generate quick mana and board advantage, while also incorporating disruptive elements like Force of Will and Chalice of the Void to control the game. Emry, Lurker of the Loch acts as a recurring threat and allows for card advantage through artifacts like Mishra's Bauble and Urza's Bauble. Urza's Saga provides additional value by tutoring for key artifacts, creating a versatile and aggressive deck that aims to overwhelm opponents with a mix of artifacts and disruptive spells.

The deck focuses on using artifacts to control the game and create a prison-like board state. It utilizes cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Trinisphere to restrict opponents' options, while also ramping up mana with cards like Urza's Workshop and Urza's Tower. The key strategy involves locking down the opponent's plays with cards like The One Ring and Planar Nexus, before overwhelming them with powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. The deck also includes ways to protect itself with cards like Pithing Needle and Ugin, Eye of the Storms.

The main concept of this deck is to use aggressive artifact creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The deck utilizes powerful artifacts like Urza's Saga and Mishra's Bauble to generate mana and draw cards efficiently. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Pinnacle Emissary provide additional value by recurring artifacts and digging for key pieces. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Lavaspur Boots offer disruption and control elements to keep the opponent on the back foot. Overall, this deck aims to establish a strong board presence early on and maintain pressure throughout the game.

The deck focuses on playing powerful Eldrazi creatures quickly and efficiently, using cards like Cavern of Souls and Eldrazi Temple to accelerate mana production. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer, while applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Reality Smasher and Eldrazi Obligator. The deck also includes cards like Wasteland and Wastes to disrupt the opponent's mana base and control the battlefield. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of speed, disruption, and powerful threats.

The main objective of this deck is to quickly get out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield through the use of Show and Tell or Sneak Attack. By leveraging cards like Omniscience and Lotus Petal, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with big threats early in the game while also having counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce to protect its combo pieces. The inclusion of card draw and manipulation spells like Brainstorm and Ponder helps ensure consistency in executing its game plan.

This deck focuses on utilizing various spells and artifacts to generate a large storm count in order to cast powerful spells like Tendrils of Agony or Ad Nauseam for a game-winning combo. By carefully sequencing spells and managing resources like Lion's Eye Diamond and Lotus Petal, the deck aims to chain together a series of spells to generate lethal amounts of damage in a single turn. The inclusion of cards like Infernal Tutor, Burning Wish, and Beseech the Mirror provide the deck with ways to tutor for key pieces needed to execute the combo efficiently and effectively.

The deck focuses on assembling a fast and efficient storm combo, utilizing cards like Dark Ritual, Lion's Eye Diamond, and Lotus Petal to generate mana quickly. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress while searching for key pieces with cards like Infernal Tutor and Wishclaw Talisman. The win condition is typically Tendrils of Agony, and the deck can also utilize Burning Wish to access additional cards from the sideboard. The deck aims to win the game in a single explosive turn by chaining together spells to generate a lethal amount of storm count.

The key concept of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to quickly ramp up mana using cards like Lotus Petal, Eldrazi Temple, and Ancient Tomb in order to deploy powerful Eldrazi creatures such as Thought-Knot Seer and Reality Smasher ahead of schedule. The deck utilizes disruptive strategies like Chalice of the Void and Kozilek's Command to disrupt opponents' game plans while overwhelming them with aggressive threats like Eldrazi Linebreaker and Glaring Fleshraker. The goal is to establish board control early and close out the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

This deck focuses on locking down the opponent by utilizing powerful artifacts like Urza's Saga, Candelabra of Tawnos, The One Ring, and Planar Nexus. Karn, the Great Creator is used to search for key artifacts, while Grim Monolith and Urza's Workshop provide mana acceleration. Trinisphere and Disruptor Flute disrupt the opponent's plays, while cards like Pithing Needle and Kozilek's Command provide additional control. The win condition is to ultimately drop Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Ugin, the Eye of the Storms to finish off the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to ramp up quickly using cards like Eldrazi Temple and Eye of Ugin to cheat out powerful Eldrazi creatures such as It That Heralds the End and Reality Smasher. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan by using Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer, while also applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Eldrazi Linebreaker and Glaring Fleshraker. The inclusion of cards like Cavern of Souls and Lotus Petal allows for additional versatility and speed in executing the game plan. The overall strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, powerful threats.

The deck focuses on locking down opponents with artifact prison elements like Trinisphere and Karn, the Great Creator. It also utilizes powerful artifacts like Ugin, Eye of the Storm and Urza's Saga to control the game. The win condition is to ramp up mana using Urza lands and Grim Monolith to cast massive threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. Key cards like Candelabra of Tawnos and The One Ring help to generate immense mana advantage. Overall, the deck aims to control the game through powerful artifacts and disrupt opponent's strategies.

The primary focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining a steady tempo in order to quickly close out the game. By using cards like Thoughtseize, Force of Will, and Daze to control the opponent's hand and board state, while deploying threats like Brazen Borrower, Murktide Regent, and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares to apply pressure, the deck aims to keep the opponent on the back foot and prevent them from stabilizing. The deck also includes efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out to deal with opposing threats.

The deck revolves around using the combo of Show and Tell to put powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield without paying their mana cost. Omniscience provides additional value by allowing you to cast spells for free once it's on the battlefield. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will, Flusterstorm, and Veil of Summer to protect your combo pieces. Cunning Wish allows you to tutor for silver bullet cards from your sideboard, maximizing your chances of success. Overall, the strategy is to cheat big threats into play and overwhelm your opponent quickly.

The deck focuses on using cheap, efficient spells and creatures to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure. It leverages instants and sorceries like Lightning Bolt and Brainstorm to maintain tempo and card advantage, with creatures like Delver of Secrets and Murktide Regent helping to close out the game quickly. The mana base is streamlined to ensure consistency and the ability to disrupt the opponent's mana base with Wasteland. The deck aims to outpace and overwhelm the opponent before they can establish their own game plan.
